name: Margie Hyams
abstract: Marjorie Hyams (August 9, 1920 – June 14, 2012) was an American jazz vibraphonist, pianist, and arranger. She began her career as a vibraphonist in the 1940s, playing with Woody Herman, the Hip Chicks, Mary Lou Williams, Charlie Ventura, and George Shearing.

concerts.label: Horizons in Jazz featuring Dizzy Gillespie, George Shearing, and Others

about_quotes.speaker: John Levy
about_quotes.evidence: the original George Shearing Quintet — which was Denzel Best, Margie Hyams, Chuck Wayne, George Shearing and myself.
about_quotes.doc_title: John Levy interviewed by Monk Rowe, Majesty of the Seas, May 31, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: John Malachi
about_quotes.evidence: The vibraharpist was a girl... Marjorie Hyams. And Chuck Wayne was the guitarist. ... it turned out to be a very enjoyable engagement that we had there.

about_quotes.speaker: Hal Galper
about_quotes.evidence: the record that got me started, “Changing With the Times” — Margie — who was it, Chuck Wayne, Margie Hyams, that band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Hal Galper interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, June 27, 2022
